{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56520","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56520/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56520/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56520/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56520","id":2647538643,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6dzjvT","number":56520,"title":"Julia on servers without IPv4","user":{"login":"tk3369","id":1159782,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNTk3ODI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1159782?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369","html_url":"https://github.com/tk3369","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tk3369/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-10T19:11:04Z","updated_at":"2024-11-10T19:11:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I am trying to build julia 1.11.1 on a server that only has IPv6 interfaces. I was able to build the julia binary but the networking related tests were skipped. And, they were skipped because `getipaddr()` throws an exception:

```julia
julia> getipaddr()
ERROR: No networking interface available
```

This is where it's invoked from the test:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/8f5b7ca12ad48c6d740e058312fc8cf2bbe67848/test/choosetests.jl#L188-L202

I do have IPv6 addresses:
```
julia> getipaddrs()
3-element Vector{IPAddr}:
...
```

The documentation suggest that it should return an IP address, just having a preference of IPv4 over IPv6. 
```julia
help?> getipaddr
search: getipaddr getipaddrs getpid getindex getalladdrinfo

  getipaddr() -> IPAddr

  Get an IP address of the local machine, preferring IPv4 over IPv6. Throws if no addresses are available.

  getipaddr(addr_type::Type{T}) where T<:IPAddr -> T

  Get an IP address of the local machine of the specified type. Throws if no addresses of the specified type are available.

  This function is a backwards-compatibility wrapper around getipaddrs. New applications should use getipaddrs instead.

  Examples
  ≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡

  julia> getipaddr()
  ip"192.168.1.28"
  
  julia> getipaddr(IPv6)
  ip"fe80::9731:35af:e1c5:6e49"

  See also getipaddrs.
```

So, why doesn't getipaddr() return IPv6 address instead? Do you consider this a bug or an error in documentation? I feel that it's the former.
